meta.title: Normies Shouldn't Have to Deal: The Hidden Costs of Janky UX

transcript: Hello everyone, my name is Caitlin Cook. I'm the Director of Growth at Moonwalk Fitness. Moonwalk is using the power of financial incentives to solve for one of the biggest problems we've seen historically when it comes to health and wellness, which is accountability. And we're building this at a really exciting time. Not only has there been a massive build-out in recent years of infrastructure from Solana and other ecosystems to support consumer apps that are more mainstream friendly, we've also seen a societal shift in several ways towards what we're building at Moonwalk with an emphasis on uh a growth of uh focus on living healthier and a desire for third spaces, in-person connection, and getting off of our phones, which we could all use. We've seen attempts at products to solve for this problem broadly in the past, both on the traditional side and in the crypto world. And on the traditional side, we've often seen more social features and gamification but lacking in financial incentives. And on the crypto side, there have been very successful move-to-earn products of the past that were ultimately unsustainable from the rewards mechanisms that they had in place, and that ultimately led to them uh kind of falling off a bit in usage over time. So let's talk about what we did with Moonwalk. Moonwalk took a simple approach to gamifying fitness, again using a blend of financial and social incentives to help hold users accountable to daily movement goals, moving more daily as an easy proxy for activity to benefit your physical and mental health. We did this by creating step-based contests where you put money on the line, whether that's USDC, Solana, or BONK, with a game with a daily step goal. If you hit the daily step goal every day throughout the contest, you get all of your money back at the end of the game and split a prize pool. That prize pool is made up of partial deposits from others in the game who missed the daily goal one or several times. This is a scalable rewards model, unlike what we've seen before with other move-to-earn apps, not using an in-app token specifically and rather leveraging the most widely held and liquid assets on Solana as the entry deposits for games. The iOS and Android app launched in January and February respectively this year. And from early on, we saw um really positive signs for the baseline product and MVP. We have around 11,000 daily active users today, and the roundabout uh data for the number of users who join their first game and go on to join a second is nearly 50%, which is extremely positive. We have a global community of users from every age group, geography, financial situation, and fitness background. And as you can see, we have a lot of in-person activations as well, bringing that connection together uh to live healthy with one another rather than just individually and on our screens. But the memecoin mania and Solana blockspace explosion uh actually exposed some UX friction in the product that we are now working to remedy in a pretty large way. Uh originally when we added more mainstream friendly sign-in methods via Privy—phone number, email, Apple, Google Auth—we wanted to mask parts of the experience that were more crypto-forward: paying for transaction fees to deposit to games and claim your prizes at the end, uh and covering the token accounts for Solana rent on the backend for the users that signed up with those more normie sign-in methods to remove that friction from the process. As the price of SOL went up, uh that obviously increased a bit of friction. There was congestion in the network which led to us having to pay priority fees. And at peak with 11,000 daily active users, we were paying and putting up 15 SOL per day to cover the costs for users that signed up with those mainstream methods rather than connecting their own software wallet. So now we're taking another path forward with brand new infrastructure for Moonwalk which is coming soon, which is really a compromise between what's worked in traditional systems and the unique benefits that blockchain can provide to a mobile app experience without hindering and rather really enhancing what the user is going through when using your product, reducing friction on the frontend and just building a better experience by blending the best of both worlds. We'll be having automated rewards distribution, one-click game signups, free-to-play experience which has been massively successful in the early trials that we've had so far, uh and also allowing for wallet sign-in for Web3 users, uh securing funds in smart contracts during games for security uh and using on-chain treasury management as well. In the future, it's not going to be crypto apps and consumer apps. It's just going to be apps, and the best experience will win. Download Moonwalk on the iOS uh App Store or Google Play and let's get moving. Thanks guys.

sources.title: Bitcoin Munari Breaks Barriers With Solana-Based Launch and $0.10 Initial Round Price
sources.snippet:
[Accessibility: Skip TopNav](https://www.globenewswire.com/www.globenewswire.com#maincontainer)

# Bitcoin Munari Breaks Barriers With Solana-Based Launch and $0.10 Initial Round Price

November 22, 2025 07:00 ET\| Source:
[Bitcoin Munari](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/Bitcoin%2520Munari)Bitcoin Munari

_Bitcoin Munari’s public presale is underway at a $0.10 initial round price as the project activates its Solana-based launch framework and advances its multi-stage development roadmap._

HELSINKI, Nov. 22, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bitcoin Munari is progressing through its public presale at an initial access price of $0.10, initiating the first round of a ten-stage distribution model that extends to the SPL release on January 20, 2026. The active phase introduces BTCM within a Solana environment designed to support early transaction execution, presale distribution, and community onboarding ahead of the project’s migration to an independent Layer-1 chain in 2027.

**Solana Deployment Establishes Operational Starting Point**

Bitcoin Munari’s first phase centers on the Solana network, leveraging its execution performance and ecosystem tooling to anchor the project’s initial functionality. The SPL deployment provides low-latency settlement, predictable transaction conditions, and a consistent entry environment for presale participants.

This foundation supports the initial stages of the multi-year roadmap and aligns with the MUNARI principles — Modern, Unified, Network, Autonomous, Resilient, Infrastructure — which define the system’s long-horizon architectural structure.

**Presale Structure Organized Around Fixed, Transparent Pricing**

The presale allocates 53% of the project’s 21 million BTCM supply across ten rounds ranging from $0.10 to $3.00. Round 1 remains active at $0.10, followed by sequential price increases through each subsequent stage.

All presale tokens unlock at the time of the SPL launch on January 20, 2026, without vesting schedules or delayed release intervals. This approach preserves clear pricing boundaries and ensures the entire distribution operates under the same fixed-supply model outlined in the project documentation.

**Integration Framework Guides Transition to Mainnet**

Development continues toward the public testnet scheduled for 2026, which will introduce validator onboarding, performance benchmarking, delegated staking simulations, and verification of the Solana-to-Mainnet migration bridge.

The migration process will use a 1:1 exchange mechanism enabling SPL tokens to transition to native BTCM once the Layer-1 chain launches in 2027.

_“A structured, multi-phase design gives each part of the system a defined function before it becomes part of the mainnet,”_ a Bitcoin Munari spokesperson said. _“The Solana deployment provides immediate operational support, while the upcoming testnet offers the environment needed for system-wide evaluation.”_

**Multi-Network Architecture Differentiates Project Model**

Bitcoin Munari’s operational sequence separates token distribution, early ecosystem access, and long-term network governance into distinct phases.

The Solana environment manages initial issuance and liquidity, while the dedicated Layer-1 chain will introduce consensus controls, validator governance, native smart contract execution, and protocol-level privacy tools.

This strategy contrasts with single-chain deployments that consolidate distribution and long-term operations within a single release.

**Independent Assessments Reinforce Early-Stage Transparency**

Technical components associated with the Solana deployment have undergone external review. Completed assessments include the [Solidproof](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=pgS9H-Q-hcMI0uhRaeVdZNiHsR-bkzNcr9EikU_rCz0CEECTTiRmJluX5rm5GUn5g0fZxEKvmETWhaKAgA5Jboprd9zBpiFKnbZntnPXOno5_9CQ2ivXfMAJx7ihN_Jm) smart contract audit, the [Spy Wolf](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=sv719ORKaUaGNB4hhJwSaabT9mEx-Nh

sources.title: Infrastructure Breakthrough as Bitcoin Munari Completes First Solana-to-Mainnet Integration Framework
sources.snippet:
[Accessibility: Skip TopNav](https://www.globenewswire.com/www.globenewswire.com#maincontainer)

# Infrastructure Breakthrough as Bitcoin Munari Completes First Solana-to-Mainnet Integration Framework

November 20, 2025 12:00 ET\| Source:
[Bitcoin Munari](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/Bitcoin%2520Munari)Bitcoin Munari

_Bitcoin Munari’s public presale is underway at the initial price of $0.35 as the project details the first iteration of its Solana-to-Mainnet integration framework, marking a key milestone in its multi-phase technical roadmap._

HELSINKI, Finland, Nov. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bitcoin Munari is progressing through its public presale at the initial $0.01 round, part of a ten-stage distribution cycle that runs through the project’s January 20, 2026 SPL token release. The presale phase coincides with the completion of the project’s first Solana-to-Mainnet integration framework, establishing the structural foundation for its transition to an independent Layer-1 blockchain in 2027.

**Solana Integration Framework Establishes Transition Structure**

Bitcoin Munari’s initial deployment on Solana provides an execution environment for early operations, liquidity access, and user onboarding. The newly completed integration framework outlines the technical path connecting this phase to the upcoming mainnet, including migration logic, validator-side tools, and token-mapping systems.

The broader roadmap aligns with the MUNARI principles — Modern, Unified, Network, Autonomous, Resilient, Infrastructure — which define the project’s long-term architectural direction.

**Presale Progress Anchored in Fixed-Supply Distribution**

The ongoing presale distributes 53% of the total supply across ten fixed-price rounds ranging from $0.1 to $3.00. All allocations release simultaneously on January 20, 2026, when the SPL token goes live on Solana.

A capped supply of 21 million BTCM underpins the model, ensuring that each round follows the predetermined pricing schedule and that distribution remains limited, transparent, and consistent throughout the presale period.

**Roadmap Advances Toward 2026 Public Testnet**

Development continues toward a public testnet planned for 2026, which will include validator onboarding, stress testing, and evaluation of the migration bridge between Solana and the mainnet. Testnet participants will have access to validator software, performance tools, and cross-chain migration functions.

_“The integration framework gives us a defined pathway from the Solana phase to the Layer-1 deployment,” a Bitcoin Munari spokesperson said. “Each stage is structured to ensure continuity between the initial token operations and the independent blockchain that follows.”_

**Structural Model Defines Transition Between Networks**

Bitcoin Munari’s architecture adopts a staged sequence rather than a single-chain launch. Initial token operations, distribution processes, and early liquidity take place on Solana, while governance, consensus, and execution shift to the dedicated Layer-1 network in 2027.

The migration mechanism uses a 1:1 conversion model, allowing SPL tokens to be exchanged for native BTCM once the mainnet becomes available. The separation of phases enables each component to mature independently before forming the unified system.

**Development Oversight Enhanced Through Independent Reviews**

Technical components associated with the Solana deployment have undergone external evaluation. Third-party assessments include the [Solidproof](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=BtW4puRN3DMONP7kHHuMyBPVCGzn7YL9GWFzHoybHhMc_yqFuCSyyPrWgTJYTulKGvjRpcPPWTJYC5m1DoK8BqGm0KgFQsmyDiJLITw9b4IVhbbqIRF8p1MnyNi4T6VC) smart contract audit, the [Spy Wolf](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=6i31MkC_vRgN39c-c0oWXd-xM6gRBfZQkewoLsmvGr3LU3oR3LZHP_x9bAsaTkNzB-jWlAR1EhH8yA58uq0B03x9iTApq9QvuROx-W-5XrHW8L7NOBiGVZ-lrWOHwtjfa_dBRcaRXwhyG1LG-Pj6EAHeuOkiw-LTe9S90o7Ifz4=) audit, and the [Spy Wolf KYC ve

tags.impact_reason: Moonwalk Fitness's integration with Solana, active user base, and innovative rewards model demonstrate solid traction, but the memecoin mania exposing UX friction prevents a higher score.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic treadmill as the anchor because it directly represents the 'fitness' aspect of Moonwalk Fitness. The concept of it overheating and burning through glowing particles visually narrates the story of the unsustainable '15 SOL daily subsidy burn' on their infrastructure. I included the text '-15 SOL' on a diegetic control panel to represent the specific, critical data point of the daily financial loss that prompted the company's overhaul.
visual_idea.description: A dramatic, low-angle shot of a sleek, brushed-metal treadmill in a dark, server-room environment. The central mechanism of the treadmill glows an intense orange-red, as if overheating. From an exhaust port on its side, a stream of glowing purple and green particles (representing Solana) is being violently ejected and dissipated into the air. On a small, integrated digital screen on the treadmill's console, the numbers '-15 SOL' are displayed in a stark, red, digital font. The background is a cavernous data center, with racks of hardware fading into the darkness, crisscrossed by volumetric shafts of light.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A dramatic, low-angle, vertical 3:4 aspect ratio shot captures a monumental, sleek, brushed-metal treadmill. The metallic surface exhibits intricate, fine-grain texture and subtle reflections of its dark, server-room surroundings, illuminated by cool shop lights. Its central mechanism pulsates with an intense, fiery orange-red glow, emanating a heat haze, vividly conveying an overheating state, reminiscent of vibrant neon glows. From a prominent exhaust port on the treadmill's side, a violent, high-velocity stream of luminous, ethereal purple and vibrant green particles, representing Solana, forcefully ejects and rapidly dissipates into the cool, dark air, appearing like vibrant neon elements. On an integrated digital screen embedded in the treadmill's console, the glowing, stark red digital characters '-15 SOL' are clearly displayed, rendered as physically existing light forms. The background recedes into a vast, cavernous data center, filled with towering racks of complex hardware that gradually vanish into profound darkness. The scene is dramatically illuminated by powerful, crisp volumetric shafts of light that slice through the dark, dust-filled atmosphere, crisscrossing the server racks and highlighting the industrial sci-fi aesthetic, with sharp, well-defined shadows contributing to three-dimensionality. The composition emphasizes the treadmill's towering presence, leaving ample negative space above to enhance its epic scale and verticality. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, dramatic volumetric lighting, realistic materials, industrial sci-fi.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic treadmill as the anchor because it directly represents the 'fitness' aspect of Moonwalk Fitness. The concept of it overheating and burning through glowing particles visually narrates the story of the unsustainable '15 SOL daily subsidy burn' on their infrastructure. I included the text '-15 SOL' on a diegetic control panel to represent the specific, critical data point of the daily financial loss that prompted the company's overhaul.
visual_idea.description: A tight, macro shot focusing on the glowing, overheating core of the futuristic treadmill. We see the intricate details of the brushed metal casing warping slightly from the heat. The stream of glowing purple and green particles is the main subject, erupting from a complex, grid-like exhaust port. The intense orange light from within casts harsh shadows and specular highlights on the surrounding mechanical components and exposed circuitry.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A tight, macro shot, 1:1 aspect ratio, centered and symmetrical. The glowing, overheating core of a futuristic treadmill fills the frame, showcasing intricate details. The detailed brushed metal casing is visibly warping slightly from the intense heat, reflecting dramatic lighting. A vibrant stream of glowing purple and green particles erupts forcefully from a complex, grid-like exhaust port, forming the central subject. Intense volumetric orange light emanating from within the core casts harsh, deep shadows and striking specular highlights across the surrounding mechanical components and intricate glowing exposed circuitry. This is a photorealistic, industrial sci-fi aesthetic, with ultra-sharp digital render and detailed textures. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed.

editorial.headline: Moonwalk Fitness Overhauls Infrastructure to Eliminate 15 SOL Daily Subsidy Burn
editorial.standfirst: The Solana-based fitness app is pivoting from a fully subsidized "account abstraction" model after network fees eroded unit economics. The move signals a broader shift in consumer crypto toward hybrid architectures that balance user experience with sustainability.
key_takeaways: **Moonwalk Fitness** reached 11,000 DAU but burned 15 SOL/day subsidizing gas for users onboarded via **Privy**.
The protocol, backed by **Hack VC** and **Binance Labs**, is deploying new infrastructure to automate rewards and curb rent costs.
Updates include a hybrid model supporting both "free-to-play" users and on-chain wagering with **USDC**, **SOL**, and **BONK**.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The promise of "account abstraction"—hiding the blockchain entirely from users—has hit a hard economic wall for **Moonwalk Fitness**. Despite achieving significant traction with 11,000 Daily Active Users (DAU) following its iOS and Android launches in early 2025, the protocol’s growth exposed a critical flaw in the "subsidize everything" model. As network activity surged, the cost to cover transaction fees and rent for users logging in via **Privy** (using mainstream methods like Gmail or Apple ID) spiked to 15 SOL per day.

This "hidden tax" of mass adoption forces a strategic pivot. While the app successfully masked the complexities of **Solana** for non-native users, the operational overhead of paying priority fees during congestion events proved unscalable. The protocol is now rolling out a new infrastructure specifically designed to compromise between the ease of Web2 and the efficiency of Web3. This includes automated reward distribution and a shift away from manual backend management of user accounts, ensuring that the cost of growth does not outpace revenue.

The overhaul also introduces a "free-to-play" tier alongside its core "skin-in-the-game" mechanics, where users wager **USDC**, **SOL**, or **BONK** on their physical activity. By segmenting the user base, **Moonwalk** aims to preserve the seamless onboarding that drove its 50% retention rate while reintroducing wallet connectivity for power users who can manage their own gas. The move represents a maturing of the "consumer crypto" thesis: moving beyond the binary of "crypto-native" vs. "normie" toward a hybrid model where the technology is invisible but the economics remain viable.
